zeta_unshifted_GRH_baseline.interpretation: Q is essentially the zero matrix to numerical precision; all eigenvalues at the dps=80 noise floor (~10^-35 to 10^-50). This validates that the reconstructed Weil quadratic form Q = M_zeros - M_arith for ζ is PSD as expected under RH, with the explicit-formula identity holding to ~35 decimal places.

zeta_shifted_positive_control.interpretation: A single zero of ζ moved off the critical line by β=0.3085 in the imaginary direction induces a rank-2 perturbation of Q: one large negative eigenvalue (~-4e4) and one large positive eigenvalue (~+2e5), while the other 10 eigenvalues remain at the noise floor (~10^-37 or smaller). The detector cleanly identifies the GRH violation.

control_L_chi_4_mod_5.reason: The Weil explicit formula for non-self-dual Dirichlet L-functions requires reconciling Fourier-transform / archimedean-factor conventions across multiple references (Iwaniec-Kowalski vs Bober-Conrey-Farmer Lemma 4.1) which could not be unambiguously resolved within runtime. Direct verification on a Gaussian test function showed a ~0.18 systematic residual against the LMFDB-stored zero list, indicating an unresolved constant/normalization mismatch in the candidate reconstructions. Reporting M_arith for L(χ4) without this resolution would produce λ_min values dominated by formula-calibration error rather than zero-structure information.

control_L_Delta.reason: Same as L(χ4 mod 5) -- formula calibration for non-ζ L-functions could not be verified within runtime. L(Δ) has gamma factor Γ((s+11/2)/2)/π^{(s+11/2)/2} and a_p values from the Ramanujan tau function; without a verified reconstruction the Weil quadratic form M_arith cannot be reliably computed.
